verification.note: Age thresholds (60/80, resident-only), Section 80TTB (Rs 50,000, old-regime-only, replaces rather than stacks with Section 80TTA), Section 80DDB (Rs 40,000 non-senior / Rs 1,00,000 senior, based on the patient's age), and Section 194P (75+, resident, pension+same-account-interest-only, bank deducts full TDS) all cross-checked against multiple current FY 2025-26 / AY 2026-27 filing guides and the Income Tax Department's own AY 2026-27 help page. Section 80D senior-citizen limit (Rs 50,000) is not duplicated here; see deduction-limits.json. Confirm against official incometax.gov.in instructions before relying on this as filing advice.

values.nri_excluded_note: An NRI does not get senior/super-senior treatment at any age: no enhanced basic exemption slab (see regime-choice.json), no Section 87A rebate, and no Section 80TTB. Uses the same rules as a non-senior resident under 60, except the general Rs 2,50,000 basic exemption still applies.

interest_deduction.covers: Interest on savings accounts, fixed deposits, and recurring deposits with banks, co-operative banks, and post offices.
interest_deduction.replaces_80tta_note: Once an individual qualifies as a senior citizen (60+) in a financial year, they claim under 80TTB instead of 80TTA for that year, not both. 80TTA is Rs 10,000, savings-account interest only, available to everyone under 60.

section_80tta_comparison.applies_to: Individuals and HUFs below 60, or any senior citizen not opting to use 80TTB in a given year is not an option — 80TTB is mandatory in place of 80TTA once senior.

section_80d_cross_reference.note: The Rs 50,000 health-insurance premium limit when a senior citizen is covered is recorded in deduction-limits.json (section_80d.self_family_senior_citizen_inr) and is not duplicated here.

section_80ddb.senior_trigger_note: The higher limit is based on the age of the person being treated (self, spouse, children, parents, or dependent siblings), not the taxpayer's own age.
section_80ddb.covers: Actual expenditure on medical treatment of specified critical illnesses under Rule 11DD (e.g. cancer, chronic renal failure, specified neurological diseases, AIDS), net of any insurance or employer reimbursement.

section_194p_filing_exemption.eligible_income: Pension plus interest income from the same bank account the pension is credited into
section_194p_filing_exemption.condition: Declaration filed with a specified bank; bank computes tax after Chapter VI-A deductions and Section 87A rebate and deducts it as full and final TDS
section_194p_filing_exemption.effect: Exempt from filing a return under Section 139
section_194p_filing_exemption.note: A filing-mechanics exemption only; does not change the amount of tax owed.
